Empires & Puzzles Ukkonen Hero Review - Kalevala Event

Ukkonen brings the noise with a heavy metal lute and a toolkit designed to keep your team alive while debuffing the enemy. Is this fighter class bard still a chart-topper or just a one-hit wonder? We break down his revive potential and dispel immunity.
Ukkonen, The Red Bard: Chords of Chaos and Revival

Ukkonen is a 5-star Legendary Fire hero from the Kalevala family, released in 2023. He plays the role of a sturdy support-tank hybrid, wielding a massive kantele (Finnish harp) that looks more like an electric guitar axe. His kit is a mix of offensive debuffs and defensive sustainability, making him a nuisance to remove from the battlefield, especially given his Fighter class. While his base form has been around for a while, the release of his costume has sparked new debates about which version reigns supreme. Spoiler: the revive ability on this base form is still a very loud argument in his favor.

Ukkonen’s design is heavy metal Viking chic. He sports a magnificent blue beard that would make any dwarf jealous, and he's mid-strum on a metallic instrument that looks capable of bludgeoning a dragon. The red and black color scheme fits his Fire element perfectly, screaming "aggression" and "passion." It’s a striking image that captures the essence of the Kalevala myths—ancient power meeting raw, elemental force. He looks like he's about to drop the heaviest riff in Valhalla.
Ukkonen's base stats, sitting at 917 Power, reflect the era of his release.

Compared to modern juggernauts pushing 1300+ stats, Ukkonen's base numbers are decidedly average, if not slightly low. He leans defensively, which suits his role as a flank or tank, but without the costume bonus or limit breaks, he can feel a bit squishy against the latest sniper nukes. However, his Fighter class helps mitigate this by giving him that annoying chance to revive, which often matters more than raw HP in clutch moments.
"Chords of Distortion" is a Swiss Army knife of a skill.
230% to all is serviceable but not devastating. It’s softening damage, not finishing damage.

-40% attack for 3 turns is significant. It cripples the enemy's ability to retaliate effectively. Against Ice enemies (his natural counter), this becomes uncleansable and lasts longer, which is a fantastic anti-meta mechanic.

The Heal over Time (HoT) for the caster and nearby is decent (618 HP over 3 turns). The unique mechanic where the duration resets upon taking special skill damage makes him deceptively tanky against ability-heavy teams.

This is the crown jewel. Making himself and nearby allies immune to buff dispels protects his HoT and any other buffs (like Taunt from a neighbor) from being wiped by the likes of Hippo or other dispellers.
speed hero, he needs 10 tiles to fire.
With a Level 23 Mana Troop or Level 11 Magic/Styx troop, you can shave a tile off.
The Kalevala family bonus reduces damage taken, but doesn't boost mana, so you are reliant on troops and emblems.
With roughly 950 attack (emblemed), a 230% hit deals roughly 2185 value before defense reduction. Spread across 5 enemies, it's good chip damage, but don't expect team wipes.
618 HP over 3 turns is 206 HP per turn. It's a steady stream, not a burst save.
gives him 15% HP and 15% Mana when dropping to low health once. This is huge. It often acts as a pseudo-mana boost, letting him fire right before dying (or after reviving).
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Kalevala family is known for being sturdy, and Ukkonen is no exception.
Receiving less damage from Ice special skills (up to -40% with 3 heroes) is excellent for a red tank/flank. It forces attackers to rely on tile damage rather than special skills to take him down.
is one of the better passives in the game. That burst of health and mana when critical can completely flip a match, especially combined with the Fighter revive talent.
fits his kit perfectly. Since he already heals over time, this adds another layer of passive healing, making him even more annoying to grind down.
Upon release, Ukkonen was a very solid tank and flank option. His ability to protect buffs made him a best friend to Taunt heroes like Ludwig.
Stat-wise, he has fallen behind. The damage of 230% is now considered low for Average speed (where modern heroes hit for 350%+ or have faster speeds).

. While his costume offers better stats, many high-level players prefer this base version on defense solely because the Fighter revive combined with his passive (Sisu) allows him to come back, gain mana, heal, and fire, potentially saving the match. He is a niche pick for defense teams specifically designed to protect a Taunt or Dodge hero.
version, but the Costume is essential context.
The costume changes him to a Sorcerer, boosts his damage to 260%, lowers the attack debuff to -30%, and adds "block reallocation" to his immunity.
The Costume has significantly better stats and a slightly more modern skill. However, losing the Fighter class (Revive) is a massive blow for a defensive hero.
Even if you use the base version, owning and leveling the costume gives massive stat boosts (Health, Attack, Defense, Mana). You absolutely want the costume bonus applied to this base card.
, the Base form (Fighter) is often superior. For
As a Fighter, Ukkonen's main job is to survive and revive.
Maximizes his survivability, making him harder to kill and increasing the value of his damage reduction passive. He needs to stay alive to keep the Dispel Immunity active for his neighbors.
His hit will be soft, serving mostly to apply the Attack Down debuff.
Makes his 230% hit sting a bit more.
He becomes too fragile for modern standards. His utility is more important than his damage output.
path is recommended. You want him reviving and casting his buffs, not trying to be a sniper.
The synergy between reviving and gaining mana/health on low HP makes him incredibly sticky.
Vital for protecting key buffs on himself and neighbors (like Taunt).
Punishes blue stacks with uncleansable attack down and reduced damage taken.
The heal duration resetting on special skill damage can keep him topped up against spammy teams.
Can be too slow against Rush teams or bad boards.
His buffs only apply to "nearby" allies, restricting team formation (he needs to be Tank or Flank).
Without the costume bonus, his base stats are a liability.
Excellent flank or tank, especially in "Equalizer" wars where his initial buffs might stick, or Rush wars where his speed is mitigated.
Great for keeping the team alive through long waves.
Ukkonen shines when he is protecting a high-value target.

Ludwig provides Taunt. Ukkonen fires and makes Ludwig's Taunt immune to dispel. Phenexa resets duration. It creates a fortress that is very hard to break without specific counters like bypassing rangers.
Ukkonen protects Hathor's buffs, ensuring the team stays overhealed and immune to ailments.
Heroes who prevent buffs (like Guardian Hippo or Mist) can stop his immunity and HoT from landing in the first place.
Fast silencers can shut him down before he gets his chords playing.
Don't pair him with heroes who overwrite his HoT with a weaker one, though his is fairly strong.

If you pull Ukkonen early, he is a fantastic tank. Focus on keeping him alive. He will carry you through map stages with his attack down and healing. Don't worry too much about the low damage; his job is to keep your hitters alive.
You likely have the costume if you are using him. The question is: Fighter or Sorcerer? If he is on your War Defense, stick to the
version. The revive chance can ruin an attacker's day, costing them a flag. Use the Costume version for offense where you can control the board and benefit from the higher alpha strike.
The community has had a mixed relationship with Ukkonen.
version as his fighter class is what makes him unbeatable in certain setups.”
- This sentiment highlights the strong preference for the base form's class.
“Throw a taunt card next to him... Fighter Ukko keeps reviving and keeps healing himself, the taunt cannot be dispelled.”
- This accurately describes his peak meta usage.
Many players were disappointed that the costume was just a "stats booster" and felt the base version's mechanics were superior for defense.

Ukkonen is a classic example of "utility over raw power." While his damage numbers have aged like milk, his utility—specifically the Dispel Immunity and Fighter class revive—has aged like fine wine. He remains a relevant, annoying obstacle on defense teams, provided you have the costume bonus to prop up his stats. He might not be the lead singer anymore, but he's the bassist that holds the whole band together.
Keep the Base version for Defense, use the Costume for Offense.
A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Ukkonen, the 5-star Fire hero from the Kalevala family. We analyze his stats, special skill Chords of Distortion, and his viability in the current meta.
